sglang.title: Refactor streaming session abort handling
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Streaming session abort refactoring
Streaming sessions had no proper abort handling — abort could corrupt session state, leak KV memory, or crash the server. This PR fixes 5 independent problems and adds comprehensive test coverage.
Design
Abort = wipe KV, keep tokens. When any streaming request is

sglang.title: [HiCache] Fix memory host free logic when share_indices_with_anchor enabled
sglang.author: huangtingwei9988
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
If share_indices_with_anchor is set to true, the entry host pool does not require explicit alloc or free.  The redundant free operation occurring here causes the memory consumed by free_slots to continuously increase.
Modifications

Accuracy Tests

Speed Tests and Profiling

Checklist

 F

sglang.title: Streaming session: fix retract tail leak via _free_tail
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Streaming session: free orphaned KV tail via _free_tail
Streaming sessions leak KV pool indices whenever the next turn's prefix_len is less than the slot's kv_allocated_len. The existing common.py spec-tail-trim only handles the speculative-decoding source. This PR replaces it with a single _free_ta

sglang.title: [lora] Speedup triton backend `sgemm` calls with better grid
sglang.author: klshuster
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
During multi-LoRA decode, each sequence gets its own segment in the Triton sgemm grid — even when many sequences share the same adapter. This means the grid scales with batch_size instead of num_adapters, launching excessive blocks and wasting GPU cycles.
This PR sorts tokens by adapter a

sglang.title: streaming session: trim spec v2 overshoot in cache_finished_req
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
Because spec accepts multiple tokens per round, the finishing round (the one whose post first trips len(output_ids) >= max_new_tokens) may commit overshoot ("bad") tokens — depending on how much of accept_lens falls past the boundary. It can also land exactly on finished_len, in which case t

For sglang-originating failures, reviews recent commits from the past day and lists suspect PRs in the report instead of auto-filing
Adds duplicate checking b

The flashinfer_cutlass FP4 all-gather path already depends on FlashInfer block-scale interleaving, so keeping a separate JIT fallback for activation quantization in standard.py was misleading and effectively dead. This change makes that dependency explicit and avoids failing with a raw Im

sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
_trim_overshoot (added in #22897) caps kv_committed_len and kv_allocated_len at target = origin + finished_len, but leaves req.swa_evicted_seqlen untouched. _free_tail (the match_prefix counterpart on the same SessionAwareCache) caps all three. The asymmetry causes a SWA pool leak when a fin

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Miles fully-async training can deadlock during the pause/update/post-process/continue-generation sequence. The failure is not one isolated bug; it is an ordering issue across three boundaries:

in-flight SGLang forward CUDA/NCCL work vs. weight-update NCCL collectives
paused scheduler TP

vllm.title: [Model] Fix Gemma 4 token repetition by dynamic BOS injection for PT models
vllm.author: lucianommartins
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR fixes the token repetition issue (e.g., "is is is is...") observed in Gemma 4 Pre-Trained (PT) models when running in completion mode (without a chat template).
The issue was caused by Gemma4ProcessingInfo.get_default_tok_params explicitly overriding add_special_tokens=False to preve

vllm.title: Update to transformers v5

vllm.bodyExcerpt:

Update Transformers pin to 5.5.3
Update Tokenizers pin to 0.22.2 (as is required by Transformers 5.0.0)
Update PEFT lower bound to 0.18.1 so that huggingface/peft@41c07f0 is included (guards import of HybridCache on Transformers version)

vllm.title: [ROCm][FEAT] Integrate aiter gemm w8a8 ptpc
vllm.author: vllmellm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR integrates aiter PTPC kernels to optimize FP8 inference performance on AMD ROCm platforms. By leveraging bpreshuffle and ck operators from the aiter library, we provide a significant performance uplift for mainstream Large Language Models compared to the standard torch.scaled_mm base

vllm.title: [compile] Invoke split FX graph by codegen.
vllm.author: zhxchen17
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
This PR reduces inference loop runtime overhead by codegen-ing slightly faster Python
code instead of invoking the FX graph directly after compilation.
Context:
Today VllmBackend returns a callable as a FX GraphModule with multiple submodules with the following code:
def forward(self, ...):

vllm.title: [Mistral Grammar] Fix tool and reasoning parsing

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When Mistral models are served with --tool-call-parser mistral and a mistral-common compatible tokenizer (tekken/v11+), #38150 introduced grammar-based tool-call enforcement: adjust_request injects a Lark grammar from mistral-common's grammar factory into structured_outputs, constraining mod

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Reject empty tools array with HTTP 400
vllm.author: jigangz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
What
tools: [] was silently accepted with HTTP 200. OpenAI returns 400 with error code empty_array for this request. This PR fixes vLLM to match that behavior.
Root cause
In check_tool_usage(), data.get("tools") returns [] which is falsy in Python, so the tool_choice defaulting logic was skipped ent

vllm.title: [perf][cpu] Accelerate BF16 GELU with LUT impl on Arm CPUs
vllm.author: fadara01
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
[perf][cpu] Accelerate BF16 GELU with LUT impl on Arm CPUs
Purpose
We noticed that PyTorch's GELU can take ~ 5% of quantized Whisper runtime, which is way too much for a non-GEMM op.
This PR introduces a Look Up Table (LUT) based implementation for BF16 GELU on Arm CPUs
It accelerates GELU by up to
